svg.title: Orbit — C3 Component Diagram (Layered View)
svg.desc: Controller, Service, and Repository layers with straight vertical call relationships
svg.text: C3 — LAYERED VIEW (1 of 2) · Standard request flow: Entry Point → Controller → Service → Repository
See c3_component_crosscutting.svg for Kafka, WebSocket fan-out, and external client wrapper flows.
Validates JWT once per request. Populates SecurityContext. No re-validation downstream.
▸ Every column above is a straight vertical path: Controller → Service → Repository. No diagonal lines, no crossing arrows.
▸ UserController and ContactService both ultimately reach UserRepository — there is no separate repository per service, only per collection.
▸ ContactService → ConversationService: on accepting a connection request, a DM conversation is created in the same transaction.
▸ GroupService → ConversationService: on group creation, a group conversation is created in the same transaction.
▸ AiService reads conversation history via MessageRepository to build prompt context — it does not write through MessageService.
▸ MessageService's full set of cross-cutting responsibilities (Kafka publish, file upload via R2, AI trigger) is shown separately
in c3_component_crosscutting.svg — this diagram only shows its standard repository call.
▸ Repositories never call other repositories. Every cross-collection consistency rule lives in the service layer — see erd.md
Service Layer Integrity Checklist for the exact ordering of each multi-step operation.
▸ AiController and AiService are scoped to Phase 4 only and can be entirely excluded from the Phase 1–3 build.
▸ This diagram intentionally omits Kafka, WebSocket, and external client wrapper components — see the companion diagram below.
